observe: Some things move too fast for either of us to referee by hand. The hardware itself has to make the promise instead.
fault: Two threads read the lock in the same breath. Without an atomic check, that was always going to collide.
repair: Watch the register trace. One operation, indivisible — that's what saves you here.
escape: Trust the atomic instruction — there's no time to check it twice.

observe: Hardware instructions like test-and-set provide atomic operations for synchronization.
fault: A non-atomic check-then-act sequence caused a race condition.
repair: Trace the atomic instruction sequence in the register log.
escape: Confirm the atomic operation completes correctly.

hints: Look for the single instruction that reads and writes the lock in one step.
